content.full_text:
Re:
Fern Polrrrcnr- PRACTTcES CoMMrssroN
.128 J Street . Suite 620 . Sacramento. CA 95811-2329
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
June 13,2008
Mario Beltran
7936 Garfteld Avenue
Bell Gardens, CA 9O2Ol
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-08-079
Dear Mr. Beltran:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding your ability to Llse
campaign funds, or establish a legal defense accollnt under Section 85304.5, to pay for
attoffrey's fees and related legal costs to defend yourself in a criminal case for alleged
misuse of campaign funds.
We need additional information about the allegations regarding misuse of
campaign funds in order to complete the analysis required in your request for advice.
Specifically, we require an explanation as to how the allegations are "directly related to a
political, legislative, or governmental purpose" and how the litigation arises directly out
of your "activities, duties, or status" as a candidate or elected officer, as required under
Government Code Section 89514.
Also, we require similar information to determine whether the criminal case arises
"directly out of the conduct of an election, campaign, the electoral process, or the
performance" of your "governmental activities and duties" as required under Section
85304. This analysis pertains to yoLlr question regarding the possibility of establishing a
Iegal defense account and using funds from this account to pay for your attorney's fees
and other related legal costs.
Please note that the Commission has advised that campaign funds may not be
used to defend against charges that do not arise directly out of the candidate or
officeholder's duties, activities, or statlls as a candidate or officeholder. For example, we
have advised that a council member who was accused of falsifying a city govelxment
health insurance document could not use campaign contributions for his legal defense.
(Breitfblder Advice Letter, No. 4-95-058.) The Commission stated this was an
irnpermissible use of campaign funds because the actions related to any employment or
any employee with health insurance and was not based on the fact that the individual was
a public official.

Mario Beltran
June 13.2008
Page 2
Therefore, whether or not attomey's fees may be paid with campaign funds or
with legal defense funds depends on the facts of each case. We lack sufficient facts to
make this determination.
Please provide additional information in writing by June 27 ,2007 so that we may
proceed with your request. If we do not receive the requested information in writing by
the date indicated, we will consider your reqllest withdrawn.
Please be advised that the 21-working days which we have to issue our response
does not begin until we receive the requested information.
If you have additional questions regarding this matter, please contact me
at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Emelyn
Commission Counsel
Legal Division
ERjgl


Fern PolrrrcAl Pnecrrcss C oMMrssroN
.128 J Street o Suite 620 r Sacramento. CA g5gl1-232g
(916) 322'5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
July 16,2008
Mario Beltran
7936 Garfield Avenue
Bell Gardens, CA 9O2OI
Re
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-08-079
Dear Mr. Beltran:
This correspondence is written confirmation that the Fair Political Practices Commission
has withdrawn your May 18, 2008, request for advice under the provisions of the political
Reform Act.l
Staff has asked you for additional information (in letters dated June 13, 200g and July 9,
2008) in response to your request for advice regarding your ability to Llse campaign funds, oi
establish a legal defense account under Section 85304.5, to pay for attorney's iees and related
legal costs to defend yourself in a criminal case for alleged misuse of camiaign funds. To date,
we have not received this information
Without the additional information, we are unable to proceed. Therefore, we have
withdrawn your request for advice and no response will be prepared.
If you obtain additional information, you may write again for advice. If you have any
questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel
By:
Commission Counsel
I The Political Retbrm Act is contained in Government Code Sections tt 1000 through 91014. All statutory
ref'erences are to the Government Code. unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fa=ir political practices
commissiOn are contained in Sections I 8 I l0 through I 1t997 of Title 2 of the califirrnia Code of Regulations. All
regulatory ref'erences are to Title 2. Division 6 of the Califirrnia Code of Regulations, unless.therwise indicater3.
